message.content:
Adversarially VERIFY this architectural claim about the codebase at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP. Re-read the relevant code yourself; do NOT trust prior summaries. Try hard to REFUTE the claim — only return CONFIRMED if the code clearly supports it. Cite file:line for every assertion.

CLAIM: A Subject can contain MULTIPLE books, and content is modeled as embedded hierarchy subject>book>chapter>topic>paragraph with NO distinct "subtopic" (подтема) entity between topic and paragraph.

Start by reading: server/src/models/Subject.model.ts, server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts, server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts

Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this architectural claim by examining the relevant code files.

content.content:
1	import mongoose, { Schema, Document, Model } from 'mongoose';
2	import {
3	  ISubject,
4	  IBook,
5	  IChapter,
6	  ITopic,
7	  IParagraph,
8	  IContent,
9	  IMetadata,
10	  Difficulty
11	} from '../types';
12	
13	/**
14	 * SUBJECT MODEL
15	 * Корневая модель для иерархической структуры образовательного контента
16	 * Содержит: Subject -> Book -> Chapter -> Topic -> Paragraph -> Content
17	 * 
18	 * Оптимизация: Использую вложенные схемы (embedded documents) вместо ссылок,
19	 * так как контент всегда читается целиком для генерации тестов.
20	 * Это уменьшает количество запросов к БД.
21	 */
22	
23	// ==================== SUB-SCHEMAS ====================
24	
25	const MetadataSchema = new Schema<IMetadata>({
26	  keywords: [{ type: String }],
27	  difficulty: { 
28	    type: String, 
29	    enum: Object.values(Difficulty),
30	    required: false 
31	  },
32	  source: { type: String }
33	}, { _id: false });
34	
35	const ContentSchema = new Schema<IContent>({
36	  text: { 
37	    type: String, 
38	    required: true,
39	    trim: true,
40	    minlength: 1
41	  },
42	  pages: [{ 
43	    type: Number, 
44	    required: true 
45	  }],
46	  metadata: { 
47	    type: MetadataSchema, 
48	    required: true 
49	  }
50	}, { _id: false });
51	
52	const ParagraphSchema = new Schema<IParagraph>({
53	  order: { 
54	    type: Number, 
55	    required: true,
56	    min: 0
57	  },
58	  content: { 
59	    type: ContentSchema, 
60	    required: true 
61	  }
62	}, { timestamps: false });
63	
64	const TopicSchema = new Schema<ITopic>({
65	  title: {
66	    type: String,
67	    required: true,
68	    trim: true,
69	    minlength: 1,
70	    maxlength: 200
71	  },
72	  /** Порядок темы в главе (drag-and-drop). Необязателен для старых данных. */
73	  order: {
74	    type: Number,
75	    min: 0
76	  },
77	  /** Темы КТП (KtpCatalog.topics._id), на которые замаплена эта тема книги. M:N. */
78	  ktpTopicIds: [{ type: Schema.Types.ObjectId }],
79	  paragraphs: [ParagraphSchema]
80	}, { timestamps: false });
81	
82	const ChapterSchema = new Schema<IChapter>({
83	  title: { 
84	    type: String, 
85	    required: true,
86	    trim: true,
87	    minlength: 1,
88	    maxlength: 200
89	  },
90	  order: { 
91	    type: Number, 
92	    required: true,
93	    min: 0
94	  },
95	  topics: [TopicSchema]
96	}, { timestamps: false });
97	
98	const BookSchema = new Schema<IBook>({
99	  title: { 
100	    type: String, 
101	    required: true,
102	    trim: true,
103	    minlength: 1,
104	    maxlength: 300
105	  },
106	  author: { 
107	    type: String,
108	    trim: true,
109	    maxlength: 200
110	  },
111	  contentLanguage: {
112	    type: String,
113	    trim: true,
114	    maxlength: 80
115	  },
116	  chapters: [ChapterSchema]
117	}, { timestamps: false });
118	
119	// ==================== MAIN SCHEMA ====================
120	
121	type ISubjectDocument = Document &
122	  Omit<ISubject, '_id'> & {
123	    getBookContent(bookId: string): string;
124	    getChapterContent(bookId: string, chapterId: string): string;
125	    getTopicsContent(bookId: string, chapterId: string, topicIds: string[]): string;
126	  };
127	
128	const SubjectSchema = new Schema<ISubjectDocument>({
129	  title: { 
130	    type: String, 
131	    required: true,
132	    trim: true,
133	    unique: true,
134	    minlength: 1,
135	    maxlength: 200,
136	    index: true
137	  },
138	  description: { 
139	    type: String,
140	    trim: true,
141	    maxlength: 1000
142	  },
143	  /** Основные предметы (общие) vs профильные (для выбора пары ЕНТ) */
144	  subjectKind: {
145	    type: String,
146	    enum: ['main', 'profile'],
147	    default: 'main',
148	    index: true
149	  },
150	  books: [BookSchema]
151	}, { 
152	  timestamps: true,
153	  collection: 'subjects'
154	});
155	
156	// ==================== INDEXES ====================
157	// Индексы для быстрого поиска по вложенным структурам
158	SubjectSchema.index({ 'books._id': 1 });
159	SubjectSchema.index({ 'books.chapters._id': 1 });
160	SubjectSchema.index({ 'books.chapters.topics._id': 1 });
161	
162	// ==================== METHODS ====================
163	
164	/**
165	 * Метод для получения текста всей книги
166	 * Используется для генерации тестов по всей книге
167	 */
168	SubjectSchema.methods.getBookContent = function(bookId: string): string {
169	  const book = this.books.id(bookId);
170	  if (!book) return '';
171	  
172	  return book.chapters
173	    .flatMap((chapter: IChapter) => chapter.topics)
174	    .flatMap((topic: ITopic) => topic.paragraphs)
175	    .map((paragraph: IParagraph) => paragraph.content.text)
176	    .join('\n\n');
177	};
178	
179	/**
180	 * Метод для получения текста конкретной главы
181	 * Используется для генерации тестов по главе
182	 */
183	SubjectSchema.methods.getChapterContent = function(bookId: string, chapterId: string): string {
184	  const book = this.books.id(bookId);
185	  if (!book) return '';
186	  
187	  const chapter = book.chapters.id(chapterId);
188	  if (!chapter) return '';
189	  
190	  return chapter.topics
191	    .flatMap((topic: ITopic) => topic.paragraphs)
192	    .map((paragraph: IParagraph) => paragraph.content.text)
193	    .join('\n\n');
194	};
195	
196	/**
197	 * Метод для получения текста по выбранным темам
198	 * Используется для генерации тестов по конкретным темам
199	 */
200	SubjectSchema.methods.getTopicsContent = function(bookId: string, chapterId: string, topicIds: string[]): string {
201	  const book = this.books.id(bookId);
202	  if (!book) return '';
203	  
204	  const chapter = book.chapters.id(chapterId);
205	  if (!chapter) return '';
206	  
207	  return chapter.topics
208	    .filter((topic: ITopic) => topicIds.includes(topic._id!.toString()))
209	    .flatMap((topic: ITopic) => topic.paragraphs)
210	    .map((paragraph: IParagraph) => paragraph.content.text)
211	    .join('\n\n');
212	};
213	
214	// ==================== MODEL ====================
215	
216	export const Subject: Model<ISubjectDocument> = mongoose.model<ISubjectDocument>('Subject', SubjectSchema);
217

content.text: I have read all three primary files. Now let me verify the type definitions to confirm there's no "subtopic" entity, and search the broader codebase for any "subtopic"/"подтема" concept that might contradict the claim.

input.command: cd /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP && echo "=== ITopic / IBook / IChapter / IParagraph type defs ===" && grep -rn "interface ITopic\|interface IBook\|interface IChapter\|interface IParagraph\|interface ISubject" server/src/types/ 2>/dev/null; echo "=== grep for subtopic / подтема anywhere ===" && grep -rin "subtopic\|sub-topic\|sub_topic\|подтема\|subTopics" server/src --include=*.ts | head -50

cd /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP && echo "=== type files location ===" && find server/src/types -name "*.ts" | head; echo "=== full ITopic context ==="; grep -rn -A 20 "interface ITopic " server/src/types/

content.text: Let me read the full type definitions for IBook, ISubject, and IParagraph to complete the verification, and run a corrected grep for any subtopic concept.

input.command: cd /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP && echo "=== subtopic / подтема across whole repo (ts/tsx) ===" && grep -rin "subtopic\|sub-topic\|sub_topic\|подтема\|подтемы\|subTopics" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" . | grep -v node_modules | head -50; echo "=== exit done ==="

content.content:
=== subtopic / подтема across whole repo (ts/tsx) ===
server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts:396:      '- По умолчанию ОДИН урок. Если материала очень много — раздели на 2–4 последовательных урока по подтемам.',
=== exit done ===

content.text:
The evidence is comprehensive. Let me note the single "подтемам" hit is in a roadmap AI prompt string (a lesson-splitting instruction for the LLM), not a content hierarchy entity. The claim is fully verified.

The hierarchy is confirmed: `ISubject.books: IBook[]` (multiple books), and embedded chain Book → Chapter (`chapters: IChapter[]`) → Topic (`topics: ITopic[]`) → Paragraph (`paragraphs: IParagraph[]`) → Content, with no intermediate subtopic entity between Topic and Paragraph.

input.claim: A Subject can contain MULTIPLE books, and content is modeled as embedded hierarchy subject>book>chapter>topic>paragraph with NO distinct "subtopic" (подтема) entity between topic and paragraph.
evidence: server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:150 — main SubjectSchema field `books: [BookSchema]` is an array, so a Subject holds MULTIPLE books.
server/src/types/index.ts:67-73 — `interface ISubject { ... books: IBook[]; }` confirms the multi-book array at the type level.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:16 — file comment states the chain explicitly: `Содержит: Subject -> Book -> Chapter -> Topic -> Paragraph -> Content`.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:98-117 — `BookSchema` has `chapters: [ChapterSchema]` (embedded), no $ref/ObjectId reference, confirming embedded (not referenced) hierarchy.
server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:64-80 — `TopicSchema` has `paragraphs: [ParagraphSchema]` directly; the only fields are title, order, ktpTopicIds, paragraphs — NO subtopic array.

server/src/models/Subject.model.ts:52-62 — `ParagraphSchema` has `order` and `content: ContentSchema`; ParagraphSchema:35-50 — `ContentSchema` holds text/pages/metadata. Paragraph is the leaf above Content.
server/src/types/index.ts:38-46 — `interface ITopic { _id; title; order?; ktpTopicIds?; paragraphs: IParagraph[]; }` — topic points straight to paragraphs, no intermediate entity.

server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts:223-242 — addParagraph nests under topic via topicId (param) resolving book->chapter->topic->paragraphs.push, mirroring the exact 5-level embedded chain with no subtopic step.

Repo-wide grep for `subtopic|sub-topic|sub_topic|подтема|подтемы|subTopics` over *.ts/*.tsx (excluding node_modules) returned only one hit: server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts:396 — a Russian LLM-prompt string ('раздели на 2–4 последовательных урока по подтемам') instructing lesson splitting, NOT a schema/type entity. No subtopic entity exists in the content model.
input.nuance: Two caveats worth knowing. (1) The multi-book capability is real and exercised: addBook/importBook push onto subject.books (subject.controller.ts:161-188), but in practice methods like resolveBookContentForAI and the schema instance methods (getBookContent/getChapterContent/getTopicsContent, Subject.model.ts:168-212) operate on a single book selected by bookId — multi-book is a storage capability, queries are per-book. (2) The hierarchy actually has SIX embedded levels if you count the leaf: subject>book>chapter>topic>paragraph>content (Content is a sub-schema, Subject.model.ts:35-50). The claim's five-level chain stops at paragraph and is accurate for the structural levels; Content is the payload of a paragraph, not a peer entity. The only token resembling 'subtopic' (подтема) anywhere is inside an AI prompt string for roadmap lesson generation and has no bearing on the content schema between topic and paragraph.

content.text:
Verdict: CONFIRMED. The Subject content model is exactly as claimed — a Subject holds multiple books (`books: IBook[]`), and the content is embedded (not referenced) as subject > book > chapter > topic > paragraph, with paragraph carrying a `content` sub-schema as its leaf payload. There is no subtopic/подтема entity between topic and paragraph anywhere in the schema or types; the single repo-wide grep hit for "подтема" is an LLM prompt string in roadmap.ai.service.ts, unrelated to the content hierarchy.

Relevant files:
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/Subject.model.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/types/index.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts
